commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an internal code-mode namespace registry, QuickJS namespace bridge, docs, tests, live repro scripts, Swift protocol refreshes, and related plugin rollback cleanup.

PR surface: Source +642, Tests +535, Docs +50, Other +820. Total +2047 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows `createCodeModeNamespaceRuntime` runs before `prepareSource`, and that runtime awaits `createScope`. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label, dirty mergeability, and a security-boundary host bridge even though the ordering bug itself is narrow.

target.title: feat: add internal code mode namespaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an internal code-mode namespace registry and QuickJS bridge so host-owned namespace globals can be exposed inside `exec` while the model-visible surface stays `exec`/`wait`.
- Gate namespaces by plugin-owned run catalog tools, serialize only safe enumerable scope values, reject forged/unsafe paths, preserve namespace runtime across `wait`, and clear namespace registrations on plugin rollback.
- Route namespace function calls through the normal `ToolSearchRuntime.call` lifecycle with policy hooks, approvals, abort handling, telemetry, and suspend/resume instead of direct host callbacks.
- Document the internal namespace boundary and keep GitHub/API-style integrations as separate owner plugins; no public plugin SDK namespace API in this PR.
- Add a fake Fictions live harness plus a standalone live Docker repro comparing regular tool mode with code namespace mode.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (45 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (24 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (29 passed)
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts --tasks=1 --modes=code-namespace` (live OpenAI, code namespace risk-audit passed)
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --allow-env OPENAI_API_KEY --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- 'OPENCLAW_CODE_MODE_LIVE_TASKS=1 bash scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh'` (remote run `run_7a735aa03ef0`, live Docker passed)
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm check:architecture`
- `pnpm protocol:check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean after fixing accepted findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: code mode can expose internal host-owned namespace globals inside QuickJS while preserving normal tool policy, approval, telemetry, abort, and suspend/resume behavior. The Docker script is an explicit live source-level repro, not a cataloged release/package Docker E2E lane.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout; AWS c7a.8xlarge Crabbox run `run_7a735aa03ef0`; OpenAI Responses API with `gpt-5.4-mini` and a real `OPENAI_API_KEY` passed through Crabbox env forwarding.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted code-mode and agent-command Vitest, local live code-namespace run, remote live Docker run through `scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh`, lint, architecture, protocol check, and branch autoreview.

Evidence after fix: local code-namespace-only live run passed risk audit in 8,624 ms with 3 model turns, 2 top-level tool calls, and 1 service call. Remote live Docker proof passed both modes on the same risk-audit task: regular mode 1/1 in 2,937 ms with 4 top-level tool calls and 4 service calls; code-namespace mode 1/1 in 2,279 ms with 2 top-level tool calls and 1 service call.

Observed result after fix: namespace globals are visible only when the owning plugin id has the required catalog tools in the run; same-named tools from other owners do not expose the namespace; raw host functions are rejected; namespace calls route through catalog tool execution; forged namespace paths are rejected; namespace calls survive suspend/resume; the live Fictions namespace completed the task with fewer service calls than regular mode.

What was not tested: no real third-party plugin namespace integration, because this PR intentionally does not add a public plugin SDK namespace API. Full matrix CI is left to GitHub after the force-push.
